violations.text: An employee was observed washing his hand with gloves on. Employee was instructed to remove gloves, rewash hands and place new gloves on. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 07/01/2025

Fish was observed thawing in stagnant water. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods must be thawed in one of the following manners: under refrigeration (41 degrees Fahrenheit or less); completely submerged under running water; as part of the cooking process; or in the microwave if cooked immediately after thawing. Inspector instructed staff to keep water runnning. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 07/01/2025

A strainer and wiping cloths were observed in the hand-washing sink in the kitchen, with rubber gloves draped over faucet. A bowl was observed in the handwashing sink at the sushi bar. A hand-washing sink must not be used for purposes other than hand washing. PIC informed, items were removed. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 06/17/2025

Staff were observed washing hands in the food prep sink. Staff were observed rinsing wiping cloths in the hand washing sink at the sushi bar. A hand-washing sink must not be used for purposes other than hand washing. PIC informed. New Violation. Correct By: 06/17/2025

Sliced avocado was observed held at 64 degrees Fahrenheit on the counter. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Inspector discussed with staff, avocado was placed in refrigeration. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 06/17/2025
Working containers used for storing chemicals taken from bulk supplies were not marked with the common name of the chemical. New Violation. Correct By: 07/01/2025
ROP tuna and crawfish were observed thawing with packaging intact. Temperatures were 39 and 40 degrees; PIC disposed. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 06/17/2025

Working containers of various liquid and dry ingredients are not labeled with the common name of the food. Working containers holding food or food ingredients that are removed from their original packages, such as flour, herbs, salt, sugar, and spices must be labeled with the common name of the food. New Violation. Correct By: 07/01/2025

Packaged food containers were observed in direct contact with the food below in the prep unit; a bowl containing egg was stored in contact with cooked shrimp tempura. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 06/17/2025

Cases of recently delivered tuna were observed stored on the floor in the kitchen, awaiting to be processed by staff. Food must be stored at least 6 inches above the floor. PIC informed. New Violation. Correct By: 06/17/2025
Dishes were observed drying on saturated wiping cloths at the prep sink. PIC informed. New Violation. Correct By: 06/17/2025
Refrigerator door gaskets on the sushi low boy were observed in poor repair. New Violation. Correct By: 07/15/2025

The non-food contact surfaces of the following have an accumulation of dust, dirt, food residue, and other debris: -refrigerator door -bottom shelf of freezer -sink basins. New Violation. Correct By: 07/01/2025
The Person in charge did not assure compliance with critical code requirements. New Violation. Correct By: 01/14/2025
The establishment does not have a written procedure for cleaning vomit and diarrhea. Inspector provided RIDOH guidance. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 01/14/2025

Raw animal foods (fish) were observed stored above ready-to-eat foods (vegetables) in the reach-in refrigerator and sushi low-boys. A bowl with raw egg residue inside was observed stored over broccoli in the prep unit. Raw animal foods must be separated from raw ready-to-eat food such as vegetables during storage. Items were moved during inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 01/14/2025
Containers were observed in direct contact with the food below in the prep unit and reach-in refrigerator. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 01/14/2025

The chlorine sanitizer concentration for the sanitizing cycle of the dishwasher was 0 parts per million (ppm). Chlorine sanitizing solutions must be between 50-100 ppm. New Violation. Correct By: 01/14/2025

Proper cooling methods were not used for time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods. Per interview of staff, broth was placed hot in the refrigerator in containers greater than four inches deep tightly covered with plastic wrap. Container was removed from refrigeration and uncovered to facilitate rapid cooling. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 01/14/2025
There is insufficient refrigeration equipment/capacity to maintain a temperature of 41 degrees or below during cooling at the time of inspection. New Violation. Correct By: 01/21/2025
ROP fish and imitation crab meat were observed thawing in intact packaging. Items were 26-36 degrees; food was removed from packaging. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 01/14/2025

The dish washing machine failed to sanitize as evidenced by the test strip reading 0ppm. Machine to be serviced. All dishware and equipment to be sanitized in 3-bay sink. New Violation. Correct By: 01/21/2025

The non-food contact surfaces of the following have an accumulation of dust, dirt, food residue, and other debris: -the doors and exteriors of refrigeration units -under prep unit hood -inside bottom of reach-in freezer. New Violation. Correct By: 01/21/2025
The drain and receptacle of the 3-bay sink has an accumulation of black film. The physical facilities shall be cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. New Violation. Correct By: 01/21/2025
